CONTENT.Club career: Moussiti-Oko had a successful spell with Dunkerque in the Championnat National, and transferred to Le Havre of Ligue 2 in 2017. He made his professional debut for Le Havre in a 1–1 Ligue 2 tie with Orléans on 8 September 2017.In January 2019, Moussiti-Oko moved on loan to Quevilly-Rouen. He left Le Havre for Le Mans, newly promoted to Ligue 2, in June 2019.On 1 February 2023, Moussiti-Oko signed a year-and-a-half-long contract with Ankaragücü in Turkey.On 18 January 2024, Moussiti-Oko joined Concarneau.
CONTENT.External links: Bevic Moussiti-Oko at SoccerwayBevic Moussiti-Oko – French league stats at LFP – also available in French (archived)Bevic Moussiti-Oko at L'Équipe Football (in French)AFCON Profile
CONTENT.International career: In 2015, Moussiti-Oko made his debut for Congo's under-20 at the 2015 African U-20 Championship, making 2 appearances.Moussiti-Oko was called up the Congo senior team in May 2017 for a 2018 African Nations Championship qualification match against the DR Congo. He made his senior debut on 10 October 2019 as a second-half substitute in a friendly against Thailand that ended as a 1–1 draw.

SUMMARY: Bevic Moussiti-Oko (born 28 January 1995) is a Congolese professional footballer who plays as a forward for the Congo national team.
